subject: UX & Research
title: Touch Target Size
description: A quick, practical guide to Touch Target Size.

hook.text: Apple's guideline: touch targets must be at least 44x44 points. Smaller targets cause frustrating mistaps.

buildup.text: Fitts's Law: smaller targets take longer to hit. Users with tremors or arthritis struggle even more.

discovery.text: WCAG 2.2 requires 24x24px minimum. Google's Material Design recommends 48x48dp for comfortable use.

twist.text: Users with limited dexterity need even bigger targets. Small targets aren't just annoying — they exclude.

climax.text: Spacing matters as much as size. Two big buttons touching cause mistaps. Add gaps between elements.

punchline.text: 44px minimum, with spacing. Big targets help tremors, fat fingers, and everyone.

quiz.question: Why is spacing between touch targets as important as size?
options: Adjacent targets without gaps cause mistaps even when each target meets the size minimum
Spacing makes the interface look better
Screen readers require space between elements
